rulings.Case Title: DARLENE BALL et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Doall Industrial Supply Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/JSR)

rulings.Case Title: ANITA BISHOP VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Nacco Materials Handling Group, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. By not presenting contention discovery responses with its moving papers, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Bishop was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. The court did not consider plaintiffs¿ late-filed opposition. The evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers was not considered. = (220/JSR)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Quintec Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production, and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Roberts was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Unibestos insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the November 1958 letter (plaintiffs¿ exhibit F) and the deposition testimony of Mr. Pierce are overruled and its objections to the July 1962 letter (plaintiffs¿ exhibit H) are sustained. = (220/JSR)
